status: P0 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 3 / IDENTITY OR PRODUCT-EQUIVALENCE BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Natural amphibian heptapeptide mu-opioid receptor agonist
mechanism_summary: High-affinity mu-opioid receptor agonism associated with potent analgesic, sedative, respiratory and dependence-related opioid effects.
evidence_maturity: Foundational sequence and receptor-pharmacology evidence with animal and analytical misuse literature; no authorised human therapeutic product is established.
regulatory_headline: UNAPPROVED OPIOID PEPTIDE / MISUSE AND CONTROLLED-SUBSTANCE REVIEW REQUIRED
certification_blockers: Supplier sequence, D-amino-acid identity, purity and sterility
closure_class: POTENT MU-OPIOID PEPTIDE / RESPIRATORY AND MISUSE RISK
closure_state: PARTIAL IDENTITY CLOSURE — LITERATURE SEQUENCE AND MU-OPIOID PHARMACOLOGY ESTABLISHED; SUPPLIER STEREOCHEMISTRY, POTENCY, PURITY, STERILITY AND HUMAN-PRODUCT STATUS OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: Primary literature identifies dermorphin as H-Tyr-D-Ala-Phe-Gly-Tyr-Pro-Ser-NH2 and demonstrates high-affinity mu-opioid receptor activity. Historical small human infusion studies do not establish an authorised medicine, safe therapeutic index or supplier-product equivalence.
critical_closure_controls: D-Ala at position 2 and C-terminal amide are identity-critical
No analgesic or opioid self-use interpretation
Respiratory depression, sedation, dependence and overdose risks retained
No equivalence to morphine, fentanyl, DAMGO or an authorised opioid
No dosing, injection, infusion or reconstitution protocol
